# Derivation

<!-- generated by rr_harness/derivation.py from EQUATION_CARDS.json; do not edit by hand -->

Equation-level derivation for **2005.12667**, generated from the reproduction's equation cards (30 equations). Each block is an equation the reproduction depends on, transcribed from the paper with its source location and tagged by derivation status. For the narrative walk-through see `DERIVATION_TRACE.md` (or `METHOD_TRACE.md`).

## Equations
### EQ001_006 — LC resonator canonical quantization

*Establishes flux and charge as a canonical pair and fixes all oscillator zero-point normalizations used later.*

$$
H_{LC}=Q^2/(2C)+\Phi^2/(2L),\ [\hat\Phi,\hat Q]=i\hbar,\ \hat\Phi=\sqrt{\hbar Z_r/2}(a^\dagger+a),\ \hat Q=i\sqrt{\hbar/(2Z_r)}(a^\dagger-a),\ H_{LC}=\hbar\omega_r(a^\dagger a+1/2),\ \omega_r=(LC)^{-1/2}.
$$

status: `verified` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (1)-(6), Sec. II.A; paper_figure: Fig. 1 and its linewidth convention

Numerical form:

```
Use dimensionless hbar=1 oscillator matrices; verify the first spacings are constant and the canonical commutator holds away from the truncation edge.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::annihilation`, `code/tests/test_cqed_reproduction.py::test_jc_block_matches_eq38`


### EQ007_019 — Distributed transmission-line and cavity normal modes

*Derives CPW and 3D-cavity spectra from continuum circuit fields and boundary conditions, then introduces the nonlinear Josephson inductance.*

$$
H=\int_0^d dx[Q(x)^2/(2c_0)+(\partial_x\Phi)^2/(2l_0)],\quad \partial_t^2\Phi=v_0^2\partial_x^2\Phi,\quad u_m(x)=\sqrt2\cos(k_mx),\ k_m=(m+1)\pi/d,\ \omega_m=v_0k_m,\quad H=\sum_m\hbar\omega_m(a_m^\dagger a_m+1/2),\quad \omega_{mnl}=c[(m\pi/a)^2+(n\pi/b)^2+(l\pi/d)^2]^{1/2},\quad L_J(\Phi)=\Phi_0/[2\pi I_c\cos(2\pi\Phi/\Phi_0)].
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ001_006` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (7)-(19), Sec. II.B-D; paper_figure: Figs. 2-4; Fig. 4(b-e) requires the unavailable COMSOL project

Numerical form:

```
Generate the first CPW resonances at f_m=(m+1)f0 with kappa_m/2pi=f_m/Q and a passive Lorentzian transmission envelope; compute rectangular-mode frequencies only when geometry is supplied.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::cpw_transmission`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t005`


### EQ020_028 — Transmon Hamiltonian, Duffing limit, and SQUID tunability

*Defines the exact compact-phase transmon model, its controlled weak-anharmonic Duffing approximation, and flux tunability.*

$$
H_T=4E_C(\hat n-n_g)^2-E_J\cos\hat\varphi,\quad \langle n|H_T|m\rangle=4E_C(n-n_g)^2\delta_{nm}-E_J(\delta_{n,m+1}+\delta_{n,m-1})/2,\quad H_q\simeq\hbar\omega_q b^\dagger b-(E_C/2)b^{\dagger2}b^2,\ \hbar\omega_q=\sqrt{8E_CE_J}-E_C,\quad E_J(\Phi_x)=E_{J\Sigma}\cos(\pi\Phi_x/\Phi_0)\sqrt{1+d^2\tan^2(\pi\Phi_x/\Phi_0)}.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ001_006`, `EQ007_019` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (20)-(28), Sec. II.D-E; paper_figures: Figs. 5-6 and captions; paper_appendix: Appendix A derives the voltage-biased circuit Hamiltonian

Numerical form:

```
Diagonalize the finite charge-basis tridiagonal Hamiltonian with a convergence check in charge cutoff; for Fig. 6 choose EC and EJ at fixed plasma frequency sqrt(8EC EJ)/h=5 GHz.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::transmon_charge_hamiltonian`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::transmon_phase_wavefunctions`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t006`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t007`


### EQ029_034 — Transmon-resonator coupling and Jaynes-Cummings reduction

*Derives the core closed-system model of Sec. III from capacitive circuit coupling, then reduces it to the two-level Jaynes-Cummings Hamiltonian.*

$$
\begin{aligned}H&=4E_C(\hat n+\hat n_r)^2-E_J\cos\hat\varphi+\sum_m\hbar\omega_m a_m^\dagger a_m,\qquad \hat n_r=\sum_m(C_g/C_m)\hat Q_m/(2e),\\H_{\rm RWA}&=\hbar\omega_r a^\dagger a+\hbar\omega_q b^\dagger b-\frac{E_C}{2}b^{\dagger2}b^2+\hbar g(b^\dagger a+ba^\dagger),\\g&=\omega_r\frac{C_g}{C_\Sigma}\left(\frac{E_J}{2E_C}\right)^{1/4}\sqrt{\frac{\pi Z_r}{R_K}}=\omega_r\frac{C_g}{C_\Sigma}\left(\frac{E_J}{2E_C}\right)^{1/4}\sqrt{\frac{Z_r}{Z_{\rm vac}}}\sqrt{2\pi\alpha},\\H_{\rm JC}&=\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a+\frac{\hbar\omega_q}{2}\sigma_z+\hbar g(a^\dagger\sigma_-+a\sigma_+).\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (29)-(34), Sec. III.A; Eq. (29) prints a minus sign before the resonator …; formal_publication_correction: Rev. Mod. Phys. 93, 025005 (2021), Eq. (31), restores the physically required plus sign…; paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix A, Eqs. (A9)-(A10), circuit derivation beyond Cg << CΣ,Cr

Numerical form:

```
Use hbar=1. Build truncated a,b matrices. Eq. (32) is omega_r n_a + omega_q n_b - (EC/2)b†²b² + g(b†a+ba†); Eq. (34) replaces b by sigma_minus in the two-level sector.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::transmon_coupling`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::transmon_coupling_via_alpha`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::duffing_jc_hamiltonian`


### EQ035_039 — Exact Jaynes-Cummings diagonalization

*Provides the exact invariant-subspace solution behind Fig. 8 and the characteristic resonant square-root splitting.*

$$
\begin{aligned}U&=\exp\{\Lambda(N_T)(a^\dagger\sigma_- -a\sigma_+)\},\quad \Lambda(N_T)=\frac{\arctan(2(g/\Delta)\sqrt{N_T})}{2\sqrt{N_T}},\\E_{\overline{g,n}/\overline{e,n-1}}&=\hbar n\omega_r\mp\frac{\hbar}{2}\sqrt{\Delta^2+4g^2n},\quad \theta_n=\arctan\frac{2g\sqrt n}{\Delta},\\|\overline{g,n}\rangle&=\cos(\theta_n/2)|g,n\rangle-\sin(\theta_n/2)|e,n-1\rangle,\\|\overline{e,n-1}\rangle&=\sin(\theta_n/2)|g,n\rangle+\cos(\theta_n/2)|e,n-1\rangle.\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ029_034` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (35)-(39), Sec. III.B; paper_figure: arXiv v1 Fig. 8 and caption

Numerical form:

```
For each n>=1 diagonalize [[n*omega_r-Delta/2, g*sqrt(n)],[g*sqrt(n), n*omega_r+Delta/2]] and compare with Eq. (38).
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::jc_block`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::jc_analytic_energies`, `code/scripts/run_reproduction.py::reproduce_figure_8`


### EQ040_044 — Schrieffer-Wolff dispersive Hamiltonian and critical photon number

*Derives the virtual-transition energy shifts, the qubit-state-dependent cavity pull, and the occupation bound for perturbation theory.*

$$
\begin{aligned}H_{\rm disp}&\simeq\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a+\hbar\omega_qb^\dagger b-\frac{E_C}{2}b^{\dagger2}b^2+\sum_j\hbar(\Lambda_j+\chi_ja^\dagger a)|j\rangle\langle j|,\\\Lambda_j&=\chi_{j-1,j},\quad\chi_j=\chi_{j-1,j}-\chi_{j,j+1},\quad\chi_{j-1,j}=\frac{jg^2}{\Delta-(j-1)E_C/\hbar},\\H_{\rm disp}^{(2)}&=\hbar\omega_r'a^\dagger a+\frac{\hbar\omega_q'}2\sigma_z+\hbar\chi a^\dagger a\sigma_z,\quad \chi=-\frac{g^2E_C/\hbar}{\Delta(\Delta-E_C/\hbar)},\\n_{\rm crit}^{(j)}&=\frac{1}{2j+1}\left(\frac{|\Delta-jE_C/\hbar|^2}{4g^2}-j\right).\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ029_034` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (40)-(44), Sec. III.C.1; paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix B.1-B.2, perturbative derivation; paper_figure: arXiv v1 Fig. 9 and caption

Numerical form:

```
Compute each bare-state energy E(j,n)=n*omega_r+j*omega_q-EC*j(j-1)/2+Lambda_j+n*chi_j, then match it to exact eigenstates by maximum bare-basis overlap.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::transmon_dispersive_shifts`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::transmon_dispersive_energy`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::critical_photon_number`, `code/scripts/run_reproduction.py::reproduce_figure_9`


### EQ045_051 — Bogoliubov normal modes and inherited Kerr nonlinearities

*Gives an exact normal-mode diagonalization of the linear sector and shows how the transmon quartic term distributes self- and cross-Kerr nonlinearities over the dressed modes.*

$$
\begin{aligned}H_L&=\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a+\hbar\omega_qb^\dagger b+\hbar g(b^\dagger a+ba^\dagger),\quad H_{NL}=-\frac{E_C}{2}b^{\dagger2}b^2,\\U_{\rm disp}&=e^{\Lambda(a^\dagger b-ab^\dagger)},\quad \widetilde\omega_{r/q}=\tfrac12(\omega_r+\omega_q\mp\sqrt{\Delta^2+4g^2}),\\H_{\rm disp}&\simeq\hbar\widetilde\omega_ra^\dagger a+\hbar\widetilde\omega_qb^\dagger b+\frac{\hbar K_a}{2}a^{\dagger2}a^2+\frac{\hbar K_b}{2}b^{\dagger2}b^2+\hbar\chi_{ab}a^\dagger ab^\dagger b,\\K_a&\simeq-\frac{E_C}{\hbar}(g/\Delta)^4,\quad K_b\simeq-E_C/\hbar,\quad\chi_{ab}\simeq-\frac{2g^2E_C/\hbar}{\Delta(\Delta-E_C/\hbar)}.\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ029_034`, `EQ040_044` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (45)-(51), Sec. III.C.2; Eq. (51) prints K_a with an extra factor 1/2; formal_publication_correction: Rev. Mod. Phys. 93, 025005 (2021), Eq. (53), gives K_a=-(E_C/hbar)(g/Delta)^4; paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix B.3, Eqs. (B26)-(B32)

Numerical form:

```
Diagonalize the 2x2 linear frequency matrix exactly; evaluate the leading Kerr expressions only away from Delta=0 and Delta=EC/hbar.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::linear_dressed_frequencies`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::bogoliubov_kerrs`


### EQ052_058 — Black-box quantization of a multimode electromagnetic environment

*Generalizes the single transmon-resonator model to arbitrary linear mode geometries and converts classical mode participation into quantum Kerr coefficients.*

$$
\begin{aligned}H_{NL}&=-E_J(\cos\hat\varphi+\hat\varphi^2/2),\quad\hat\varphi=\sum_m(\varphi_ma_m+\mathrm{H.c.}),\\H_{NL}^{\rm RWA}&\simeq\sum_m\hbar\Delta_m n_m+\frac12\sum_m\hbar K_m a_m^{\dagger2}a_m^2+\sum_{m>n}\hbar\chi_{mn}n_mn_n,\\\hbar\chi_{mn}&=-E_J\varphi_m^2\varphi_n^2,\quad K_m=\chi_{mm}/2,\quad\Delta_m=\tfrac12\sum_n\chi_{mn},\\p_m&=(2E_J/\hbar\omega_m)\varphi_m^2,\quad\chi_{mn}=-\frac{\hbar\omega_m\omega_n}{4E_J}p_mp_n.\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ045_051` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (52)-(58), Sec. III.D; paper_figure: arXiv v1 Fig. 10, equivalent impedance and normal-mode schematic

Numerical form:

```
For vectors omega and phi, form chi=-EJ/hbar * outer(phi^2,phi^2), K=diag(chi)/2, Delta=rowsum(chi)/2, and p=2(EJ/hbar)phi^2/omega.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::black_box_kerr`


### EQ059_062 — Arbitrary multilevel artificial atom in the dispersive regime

*Removes the Duffing approximation by using exact artificial-atom eigenstates and matrix elements, while retaining a universal second-order dispersive structure.*

$$
\begin{aligned}H&=\sum_j\hbar\omega_j|j\rangle\langle j|+\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a+i\sum_{ij}\hbar g_{ij}|i\rangle\langle j|(a^\dagger-a),\\H_{\rm disp}&\simeq\sum_j\hbar(\omega_j+\Lambda_j)|j\rangle\langle j|+\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a+\sum_j\hbar\chi_ja^\dagger a|j\rangle\langle j|,\\\Lambda_j&=\sum_i\frac{|g_{ij}|^2}{\omega_j-\omega_i-\omega_r},\quad\chi_j=\sum_i\left(\frac{|g_{ij}|^2}{\omega_j-\omega_i-\omega_r}-\frac{|g_{ij}|^2}{\omega_i-\omega_j-\omega_r}\right).\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ040_044` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (59)-(62), Sec. III.E; paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix B.2, multilevel Schrieffer-Wolff transformation

Numerical form:

```
Given exact level frequencies and complex gij, sum |gij|^2 over all connected levels with the two printed denominators; reject resonant denominators rather than silently regularizing them.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::generic_multilevel_shifts`


### EQ063 — Longitudinal light-matter coupling

*Shows that longitudinal coupling displaces the resonator conditionally without mixing the qubit eigenstates.*

$$
H_z=\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a+\frac{\hbar\omega_q}{2}\sigma_z+\hbar g_z(a^\dagger+a)\sigma_z=\hbar\omega_r\left(a^\dagger+\frac{g_z}{\omega_r}\sigma_z\right)\left(a+\frac{g_z}{\omega_r}\sigma_z\right)+\frac{\hbar\omega_q}{2}\sigma_z-\frac{\hbar g_z^2}{\omega_r}.
$$

status: `verified` · source: paper_equation: arXiv v1 Eq. (63), Sec. III.F

Numerical form:

```
Diagonalize one displaced-oscillator block per sigma_z=+/-1 and compare its low spectrum with n*omega_r+sigma_z*omega_q/2-gz^2/omega_r.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::longitudinal_block`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::longitudinal_analytic_energy`


### EQ064_065 — Continuum transmission-line bath and canonical fields

*Supplies the continuum bath normalization needed to derive the microscopic system-bath Hamiltonian and the temporal input/output fields.*

$$
\begin{aligned}H_{\rm tml}&=\int_0^\infty d\omega\,\hbar\omega b_\omega^\dagger b_\omega,\quad[b_\omega,b_{\omega'}^\dagger]=\delta(\omega-\omega'),\\\Phi_{\rm tml}(x)&=\int_0^\infty d\omega\sqrt{\frac{\hbar}{\pi\omega cv}}\cos(\omega x/v)(b_\omega^\dagger+b_\omega),\\Q_{\rm tml}(x)&=i\int_0^\infty d\omega\sqrt{\frac{\hbar\omega c}{\pi v}}\cos(\omega x/v)(b_\omega^\dagger-b_\omega).\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (64)-(65); formal RMP Eqs. (66)-(67)

Numerical form:

```
No finite bath grid is used for physical claims. A single discretized bath mode is used only for the Hermiticity sanity check of Eqs. (66)-(67).
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::discretized_bath_hamiltonians`


### EQ066 — Microscopic oscillator-bath Hamiltonian

*Connects the circuit capacitance to a continuum system-bath coupling before the Markov and rotating-wave approximations.*

$$
H=H_S+H_{\rm tml}-\hbar\int_0^\infty d\omega\,\lambda(\omega)(b_\omega^\dagger-b_\omega)(a^\dagger-a),\quad\lambda(\omega)=\frac{C_\kappa}{\sqrt{cC_r}}\sqrt{\frac{\omega_r\omega}{2\pi v}}.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ064_065` · source: paper_equation: arXiv v1 Eq. (66); formal RMP Eq. (68); paper_appendix: Appendix C Eq. (C2), capacitive interaction Qr Qtml(0)

Numerical form:

```
Tensor a and b operators for a finite-mode Hermiticity audit only; open-system dynamics starts from the Markovian generator after the bath is traced out.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::discretized_bath_hamiltonians`


### EQ067 — Narrow-band rotating-wave system-bath coupling and source correction

*Applies the high-Q narrow-band and rotating-wave approximations; the formal publication repairs an arXiv-v1 sign that would make the interaction anti-Hermitian.*

$$
H_{\rm RWA}=H_S+H_{\rm tml}+\hbar\int_0^\infty d\omega\,\lambda(\omega_r)(ab_\omega^\dagger+a^\dagger b_\omega).
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ066` · source: paper_equation: arXiv v1 Eq. (67), printed as a b† - a† b; formal_publication_correction: Rev. Mod. Phys. 93, 025005 (2021), Eq. (69), printed as a b† + a† b

Numerical form:

```
Use the formal-publication Hermitian coupling. Preserve the arXiv literal operator only as a negative regression test.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::discretized_bath_hamiltonians`


### EQ068_069 — Born-Markov thermal Lindblad master equation

*Eliminates the bath under weak-coupling, stationary thermal, Born, Markov, and secular assumptions to obtain completely positive oscillator damping and heating.*

$$
\dot\rho=-\frac{i}{\hbar}[H_S,\rho]+\kappa(\bar n_\kappa+1)\mathcal D[a]\rho+\kappa\bar n_\kappa\mathcal D[a^\dagger]\rho,\quad\mathcal D[O]\rho=O\rho O^\dagger-\tfrac12\{O^\dagger O,\rho\},\quad\kappa=2\pi\lambda(\omega_r)^2.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ067` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (68)-(69); formal RMP Eqs. (70)-(71)

Numerical form:

```
Use collapse operators sqrt(kappa(nbar+1))*a and sqrt(kappa*nbar)*a† in a finite Fock basis; integrate the vectorized density matrix with tight tolerances.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::dissipator`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::lindblad_rhs`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::thermal_oscillator_evolution`


### EQ070_071 — Traveling-wave voltage and Kirchhoff boundary condition

*Turns the semi-infinite line into incoming and outgoing traveling fields linked at x=0 by current conservation.*

$$
\begin{aligned}V_{\rm in/out}(t)&=i\int_0^\infty d\omega\sqrt{\frac{\hbar\omega}{4\pi cv}}e^{i\omega t}b_{L/R,\omega}^\dagger+\mathrm{H.c.},\\I(t)&=\frac{V_{\rm out}(t)-V_{\rm in}(t)}{Z_{\rm tml}},\qquad I(t)=\frac{C_\kappa}{C_r}\dot Q_r(t).\end{aligned}
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ064_065`, `EQ066` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (70)-(71); formal RMP Eqs. (72)-(73); paper_appendix: Appendix C Eqs. (C5)-(C10), wave equation and boundary integration

Numerical form:

```
These boundary equations are derived analytically. The executable response begins after the narrow-band envelope definitions in Eqs. (73)-(74).
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::passive_one_port_response`


### EQ072 — Input-output boundary relation

*Relates the measurable outgoing field to the incoming field and the field radiated by the resonator.*

$$
b_{\rm out}(t)-b_{\rm in}(t)=\sqrt\kappa\,a(t),\qquad\kappa=Z_{\rm tml}C_\kappa^2\omega_r^2/C_r.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ070_071` · source: paper_equation: arXiv v1 Eq. (72); formal RMP Eq. (74); paper_appendix: Appendix C Eqs. (C11)-(C14)

Numerical form:

```
The code uses the equivalent passive convention bout=bin-sqrt(kappa)*a. Changing the resonator or port phase flips the displayed sign but not observables; the relative sign with Eq. (75) is fixed by passivity.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::passive_one_port_response`


### EQ073_074 — Narrow-band temporal input and output fields

*Defines slowly varying time-domain quantum-noise fields with flux normalization appropriate for input-output theory.*

$$
b_{\rm in/out}(t)=\frac{-i}{\sqrt{2\pi}}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}d\omega\,b_{L/R,\omega}e^{-i(\omega-\omega_r)t},\qquad[b_{\rm in/out}(t),b_{\rm in/out}^\dagger(t')]=\delta(t-t').
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ064_065`, `EQ070_071` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (73)-(74); formal RMP Eqs. (75)-(76)

Numerical form:

```
The steady-state response is evaluated directly in detuning space; no artificial finite-time delta approximation is used for acceptance claims.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::passive_one_port_response`


### EQ075 — Quantum Langevin equation and passive sign convention

*Combines unitary system dynamics, radiative damping, and incident quantum noise; together with the boundary relation it determines measurable response.*

$$
\dot a=i[H_S,a]-\frac{\kappa}{2}a+\sqrt\kappa\,b_{\rm in}\quad(\hbar=1),\qquad\text{passive equivalent used numerically: }\dot a=-(i\Delta+\kappa/2)a+\sqrt\kappa b_{\rm in},\;b_{\rm out}=b_{\rm in}-\sqrt\kappa a.
$$

status: `reconstructed` · derived from: `EQ072`, `EQ073_074`, `EQ068_069` · source: paper_equation: arXiv v1 Eq. (75); formal RMP Eq. (77); paper_appendix: Appendix C Eqs. (C15)-(C20), slowly varying envelope derivation

Numerical form:

```
For a coherent input beta in the rotating frame, alpha_ss=sqrt(kappa) beta/(i Delta+kappa/2) and r=bout/bin=(i Delta-kappa/2)/(i Delta+kappa/2), hence |r|=1.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::passive_one_port_response`


### EQ076_086 — Traveling voltage, transmon decoherence, dispersive dissipation, and coherent drive

*Extends the bath model to qubit T1/T2, exposes mixing-induced Purcell channels, and connects coherent incoming fields to displacement control.*

$$
V_{\rm tml}(x,t)\simeq V_{\rm in}(t-x/v)+V_{\rm out}(t+x/v),\quad \dot\rho=-i[H_q,\rho]+\gamma(\bar n+1)D[b]\rho+\gamma\bar nD[b^\dagger]\rho+2\gamma_\varphi D[b^\dagger b]\rho,\quad T_1^{-1}=\gamma_\downarrow+\gamma_\uparrow,\ T_2^{-1}=T_1^{-1}/2+\gamma_\varphi,\quad \gamma_\kappa=(g/\Delta)^2\kappa,\ \kappa_\gamma=(g/\Delta)^2\gamma,\ \gamma_\Delta=2(g/\Delta)^2\gamma_\varphi,\quad H_d=\hbar[\varepsilon(t)a^\dagger e^{-i\omega_dt-i\phi_d}+\mathrm{H.c.}],\quad |\alpha\rangle=D(\alpha)|0\rangle.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ068_069`, `EQ072`, `EQ075`, `EQ040_044` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (76)-(86), Sec. IV.B-F; paper_figures: Figs. 12-13 provide relaxation, dephasing, Purcell, and inverse-Purcell channel context

Numerical form:

```
Integrate finite-dimensional Lindblad equations and compare population/coherence to exp(-t/T1) and exp(-t/T2); verify transformed collapse-channel rates and coherent-state normalization.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::thermal_oscillator_evolution`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::one_excitation_dynamics`, `code/tests/test_full_rmp_reproduction.py::test_pointer_trajectory_reaches_analytic_unit_population`


### EQ087_099 — Filtered microwave detection, quantum-limited amplification, and IQ quadratures

*Fixes the single-mode filter normalization, shows why a phase-preserving amplifier requires an idler mode and at least half a quantum of added noise, and maps mixer voltages to field quadratures.*

$$
b_{\rm out}[f]=\int dt\,f(t)b_{\rm out}(t),\ \int dt|f(t)|^2=1,\quad b_{\rm out}^{(1)}[f]=\sqrt G b_{\rm out}[f]+\sqrt{G-1}h^\dagger[f],\quad N_{\rm sys}=N_1+N_2/G_1+\cdots,\quad \eta=[1+2N_{\rm sys}]^{-1},\quad V_{IF}(t)=I(t)\cos(\omega_{IF}t)+Q(t)\sin(\omega_{IF}t),\quad X_\phi=(ae^{-i\phi}+a^\dagger e^{i\phi})/\sqrt2.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ073_074`, `EQ076_086` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (87)-(99), Sec. V.A; paper_figures: Figs. 14-16 define the measurement chain, noise cascade, and IQ mixer

Numerical form:

```
Propagate Gaussian signal and independent idler variances through a gain cascade; confirm commutator preservation, the half-quantum limit, and eta=1/(1+2Nsys).
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::phase_preserving_amplifier_metrics`, `code/tests/test_full_rmp_reproduction.py::test_phase_preserving_amplifier_preserves_commutator_and_adds_half_quantum`


### EQ100_106 — Wigner, characteristic, marginal, and Husimi-Q representations

*Provides the phase-space machinery used for tomography, cat-code plots, Fock superpositions, and squeezed states.*

$$
W_\rho(x,p)=2\int dx'dp'\,C_\rho(x',p')e^{2i(px'-xp')},\quad C_\rho(x,p)=\mathrm{Tr}[\rho D(x+ip)],\quad W_\rho(\alpha)=\frac{2}{\pi}\mathrm{Tr}[D^\dagger(\alpha)\rho D(\alpha)P],\quad W_{|\beta\rangle}(\alpha)=\frac{2}{\pi}e^{-2|\alpha-\beta|^2},\quad Q(\alpha)=\pi^{-1}\langle\alpha|\rho|\alpha\rangle.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ085`, `EQ086`, `EQ087_099` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (100)-(106), Sec. V.B; paper_figure: Fig. 17 illustrates quadrature marginals

Numerical form:

```
Evaluate Wigner functions from analytic Fock-basis matrix elements or displaced parity on a converged grid; require realness, unit integral, and parity bounds.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::fock_state_wigner`, `code/tests/test_full_rmp_reproduction.py::test_fock_wigner_normalizes_and_has_odd_parity_at_origin`


### EQ107_120 — Dispersive pointer states, measurement SNR, efficiency, and longitudinal readout

*Turns the dispersive Hamiltonian into a quantitative readout model and separates pointer-state distance, integrated information, backaction, and chain efficiency.*

$$
H_{disp}\simeq\hbar(\omega_r+\chi\sigma_z)a^\dagger a+\hbar\omega_q\sigma_z/2,\quad |\psi(t)\rangle=c_g|g,\alpha_g(t)\rangle+c_e|e,\alpha_e(t)\rangle,\quad \dot\alpha_{e/g}=-i\varepsilon-[i(\delta_r\pm\chi)+\kappa/2]\alpha_{e/g},\quad \alpha_{e/g}^{ss}=-i\varepsilon/[i(\delta_r\pm\chi)+\kappa/2],\quad \mathrm{SNR}^2=2\eta\kappa\int_0^\tau dt|\alpha_e-\alpha_g|^2,\quad F=\tfrac12[1+\mathrm{erf}(\mathrm{SNR}/2)],\quad \eta=\Gamma_{meas}/(2\Gamma_\varphi),\quad H_z(t)=\hbar\tilde g_z(t)(a+a^\dagger)\sigma_z.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ040_044`, `EQ075`, `EQ087_099` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (107)-(120), Sec. V.C; paper_figures: Figs. 18-19 and their parameter annotations

Numerical form:

```
Integrate the two complex pointer ODEs for finite pulses, compute phase-space distance and SNR, and compare the long-time result to the analytic steady-state formula.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::dispersive_pointer_trajectory`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::integrated_pointer_snr`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t008`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t009`


### EQ121_125 — Resonant coupling regimes, Maxwell-Bloch response, and Purcell interpolation

*Connects the resonant JC spectrum to measurable transmission in bad-cavity, bad-qubit, and strong-coupling regimes.*

$$
E_{n,\pm}=n\hbar\omega_r\pm\hbar g\sqrt n,\quad \dot{\langle a\rangle}=-i\delta_r\langle a\rangle-ig\langle\sigma_-\rangle-\kappa\langle a\rangle/2-i\varepsilon,\quad \dot{\langle\sigma_-\rangle}=-(i\delta_q+\gamma_2)\langle\sigma_-\rangle+ig\langle a\sigma_z\rangle,\quad \langle a\rangle_{ss}=-i\varepsilon/[i\delta_r+\kappa/2+g^2/(i\delta_q+\gamma_2)],\quad \gamma_\kappa=\frac{(g/\Delta)^2\kappa}{1+(\kappa/2\Delta)^2}.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ035_039`, `EQ075`, `EQ076_086` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (121)-(125), Sec. VI.A; paper_figures: Figs. 20-22; captions supply the simulation rates

Numerical form:

```
Use both the analytic weak-drive susceptibility and a finite JC Lindblad steady state/time evolution; cross-check peak locations, linewidth limits, positivity, and trace preservation.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::linear_cqed_response`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::one_excitation_dynamics`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::thermal_jc_spectrum`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t010`


### EQ126_128 — Dispersive spectroscopy, ac-Stark response, and measurement-induced dephasing

*Relates cavity photons to qubit frequency shift, power broadening, number splitting, and readout backaction.*

$$
H_{disp}\simeq\hbar\omega_r a^\dagger a+\frac{\hbar}{2}[\omega_q+2\chi a^\dagger a+\chi]\sigma_z,\quad \langle\sigma_z\rangle_{ss}=-1+[1+(\delta_q/\gamma_2)^2+\Omega_R^2/(\gamma_1\gamma_2)]^{-1}\Omega_R^2/(\gamma_1\gamma_2),\quad \Gamma_\varphi=2\kappa|\alpha_e-\alpha_g|^2/4=\kappa|\alpha_e-\alpha_g|^2/2.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ040_044`, `EQ107_120`, `EQ121_125` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (126)-(128), Sec. VI.B; paper_figures: Figs. 24-26 and captions

Numerical form:

```
Evaluate the Bloch formula and compare it to a two-level Lindblad steady state; for photon-number splitting sum number-conditioned responses with a normalized Poisson distribution.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::bloch_excited_population`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::photon_number_split_spectrum`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::duffing_cavity_pull`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t013`


### EQ129_153 — Single-qubit control and two-qubit gate Hamiltonians

*Organizes microwave and flux control as rotating-frame resonance selection, with leakage, virtual exchange, conditional phase, and parametric sidebands as variations of the same model.*

$$
H'(t)=H'_q+\hbar\varepsilon(t)(b^\dagger e^{-i\phi_d}+be^{i\phi_d}),\quad H'_{TLS}=\hbar\delta_q\sigma_z/2+\hbar\Omega_R(t)[\cos\phi_d\sigma_x+\sin\phi_d\sigma_y]/2,\quad H_{swap}=\hbar J(\sigma_+^{(1)}\sigma_-^{(2)}+\mathrm{H.c.}),\quad J=g_1g_2(1/\Delta_1+1/\Delta_2)/2,\quad H_{CZ}=\hbar J_{11,02}(|11\rangle\langle02|+\mathrm{H.c.}),\quad H_{CR}\supset-\hbar\varepsilon J E_{C1}(\hbar\Delta_{12})^{-1}\sigma_z^{(1)}\sigma_x^{(2)},\quad H_{para}\simeq\tilde J(\sigma_+^{(1)}\sigma_-^{(2)}+\mathrm{H.c.})/2.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ020_028`, `EQ040_044`, `APPB001_B032` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (129)-(153), Sec. VII.A-B; paper_figures: Figs. 28-30; only an independent DRAG simulation is reproducible without external calib…

Numerical form:

```
Propagate a three-level driven transmon under Gaussian and derivative quadratures, then compare leakage and gate error; verify analytic exchange and parametric resonance against small matrix evolutions.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::drag_pi_pulse`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t016`


### EQ154_157 — Bosonic amplitude-damping codes and cat-code basis states

*Shows how oscillator parity and separated loss spaces preserve an encoded qubit against one amplitude-damping event.*

$$
|0_L\rangle=(|0\rangle+|4\rangle)/\sqrt2,\ |1_L\rangle=|2\rangle,\quad |0_L\rangle_{4q}=(|0000\rangle+|1111\rangle)/\sqrt2,\ |1_L\rangle_{4q}=(|0011\rangle+|1100\rangle)/\sqrt2,\quad |0_L\rangle_{cat}\propto|\alpha\rangle+|i\alpha\rangle+|-\alpha\rangle+|-i\alpha\rangle,\quad |1_L\rangle_{cat}\propto|\alpha\rangle-|i\alpha\rangle+|-\alpha\rangle-|-i\alpha\rangle.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ085`, `EQ100_106` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (154)-(157), Sec. VII.C; paper_table: Table I compares uncorrected, four-qubit, and bosonic encodings; paper_figure: Fig. 31 uses alpha=4

Numerical form:

```
Construct normalized truncated-Fock codewords, compute Gram matrices of the I and a error spaces, photon-number moments, parity, and analytic Wigner functions.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::binomial_code_metrics`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::cat_state_coefficients`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t017`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t018`


### EQ158_164 — Degenerate parametric amplification, squeezing, and remote parity interaction

*Derives the quadratic engine of quantum-limited amplification and squeezing and the dispersive interaction used for remote parity measurement.*

$$
H=\hbar\omega_0a^\dagger a+(\hbar K/2)a^{\dagger2}a^2+\hbar\varepsilon_p(a^\dagger e^{-i\omega_pt}+ae^{i\omega_pt}),\quad D^\dagger(\alpha)HD(\alpha)=\hbar\delta a^\dagger a+(\hbar/2)(\varepsilon_2a^{\dagger2}+\varepsilon_2^*a^2)+H_{corr},\ \varepsilon_2=K\alpha^2,\quad S(\xi)=\exp[(\xi^*a^2-\xi a^{\dagger2})/2],\quad \Delta X_{\theta/2}^2=e^{-2r}/2,\ \Delta X_{\theta/2+\pi/2}^2=e^{2r}/2,\quad H_{parity}=\hbar\chi(\sigma_z^{(1)}+\sigma_z^{(2)})a^\dagger a.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ045_051`, `EQ100_106`, `EQ107_120` · source: paper_equations: arXiv v1 Eqs. (158)-(164), Sec. VIII; paper_figures: Figs. 32-33; theory panels have complete state parameters

Numerical form:

```
Generate squeezed-vacuum covariance and Wigner grids; verify normalization, principal variances, minimum uncertainty, and the caption's dB curves. Generate Fock-superposition Wigner functions separately from experimental tomography.
```

Code: `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::squeezed_vacuum_wigner`, `code/src/full_rmp_reproduction.py::squeezed_quadrature_variance`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t019`, `code/scripts/run_full_rmp_reproduction.py::reproduce_t020`


### APPA001_A010 — Appendix A: voltage-biased transmon and capacitive oscillator coupling

*Closes the circuit-to-Hamiltonian chain used for transmon gate charge and resonator coupling without assuming the interaction by inspection.*

$$
L=\tfrac12\dot{\boldsymbol\Phi}^{T}C\dot{\boldsymbol\Phi}+E_J\cos(2\pi\Phi_A/\Phi_0)-V_g C_g\dot\Phi_A,\quad H=\tfrac12(\mathbf Q-\mathbf Q_g)^TC^{-1}(\mathbf Q-\mathbf Q_g)-E_J\cos(2\pi\Phi_A/\Phi_0),\quad H\simeq4E_C(n-n_g+n_r)^2-E_J\cos\varphi+\hbar\omega_ra^\dagger a.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ001_006`, `EQ020_028`, `EQ029_034` · source: paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix A, Eqs. (A1)-(A10)

Numerical form:

```
Compare the exact inverse-capacitance Hamiltonian with its small-Cg expansion over a dimensionless coupling sweep.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::transmon_coupling`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::duffing_jc_hamiltonian`


### APPB001_B032 — Appendix B: BCH, Schrieffer-Wolff, Bogoliubov, and off-resonant drive

*Provides the controlled unitary-transform calculus underlying dispersive shifts, multilevel couplings, inherited Kerr terms, and driven-gate reductions.*

$$
H_U=U^\dagger HU-i\hbar U^\dagger\dot U,\quad e^SHe^{-S}=H+[S,H]+[S,[S,H]]/2+\cdots,\quad [H_0,S^{(1)}]=-V,\quad H_{eff}^{(2)}=H_0+[S^{(1)},V]/2,\quad U_\Lambda=e^{\Lambda(a^\dagger b-ab^\dagger)},\ \tan(2\Lambda)=2g/\Delta.
$$

status: `verified` · derived from: `EQ029_034`, `EQ040_044`, `EQ045_051`, `EQ129_153` · source: paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix B, Eqs. (B1)-(B32) and following driven-transmon equations

Numerical form:

```
Compare exact finite-matrix unitary transforms against second-order SW and exact 2x2 normal-mode eigenvalues across a controlled small parameter sweep.
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::transmon_dispersive_shifts`, `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::linear_dressed_frequencies`, `code/tests/test_cqed_reproduction.py::test_bogoliubov_frequencies_match_matrix_diagonalization`


### APPC001_C020 — Appendix C: transmission-line boundary derivation of input-output theory

*Derives rather than postulates the boundary condition, temporal field normalization, damping kernel, and Langevin drive used throughout measurement theory.*

$$
H_{tml}=\int dx\,\theta(x)[Q(x)^2/(2c)+(\partial_x\Phi)^2/(2l)],\quad \partial_t^2\Phi=v^2\partial_x^2\Phi,\quad V_{out}-V_{in}=Z_{tml}C_\kappa\ddot\Phi_A,\quad b_{out}-b_{in}=\sqrt\kappa a,\quad \dot a=i[H_S,a]-\kappa a/2+\sqrt\kappa b_{in}.
$$

status: `reconstructed` · derived from: `EQ064_065`, `EQ066`, `EQ070_071`, `EQ072`, `EQ073_074`, `EQ075` · source: paper_appendix: arXiv v1 Appendix C, Eqs. (C1)-(C20)

Numerical form:

```
Reuse the continuum-normalization, Hermiticity, passive-reflection, and energy-decay tests associated with Eqs. (64)-(75).
```

Code: `code/src/cqed_reproduction.py::passive_one_port_response`
